text: a traumatic brain injury or tbi is a

working together with my state and local

in this program we assess how each

and here when i say safer i mean safer

in some ways this is such a simple

we're not treating the brain injury

that gets people into all of this

we do quick neural psychological

and weaknesses in the way an inmate

using that information we write two
reports
one a report for the system
with specific recommendations on how to

the other is a letter to the inmate with
specific suggestions for how to manage
themselves
for example
if our test results suggest that a

that would be an auditory memory deficit
in that case our letter to the court

vinnie was hit by a car when he was 15
and from that moment forward spent more

with some basic skill building after our

pretty significant memory impairments
vinnie learned to use the alarm and

with basic tools like that under his

what's so striking for vinnie is that

he says now

when he heard about this program he saw

he actually sits down with all of his

to help them see that there's basically
two categories of defendants in the

the ones we're afraid of oftentimes

and the ones we're mad at
these are the ones who miss all of their

and judge bowen believes that with a

he proved that
with navy veteran mike
judge bowen saw the correlation between
mike's history of a massive 70-foot fall
and his long-standing pattern of
difficulty showing up on the right day
for court appointments
and complying with mandatory therapy

and instead of sentencing him to more

and with those supports mike's back to

he's repairing relationships with his
